mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates rising aggregate income and consumption demand, translating into higher corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not positive in more than 50% of months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6% signals substantial labor market slack that depresses wage growth and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is above 6%, S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate combined with a decrease in industrial production indicates monetary tightening into a weakening economy, jointly raising recession risk and discount rates to drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS changes and INDPRO changes fails to produce a statistically significant negative coefficient (p < 0.05) in predicting 20-day returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month acceleration in consumer price inflation signals that the central bank will raise rates further, increasing the equity risk premium and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods classified as monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month CPIAUCSL accelerations are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of occurrences over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals contracting manufacturing activity and corporate earnings pressure, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in INDPRO are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in fewer than 50% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals expanding aggregate demand and supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of quarters over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based demand strength across consumption and residential investment, jointly lifting corporate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of month-over-month RSAFS and HOUST increases fails to produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate raises short-term borrowing costs and reduces equity market liquidity, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in FEDFUNDS are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.
